documents.title: SGLang Documentation — SGLang
documents.summary: SGLang is a high-performance serving framework designed for low-latency and high-throughput inference of large language and multimodal models across various hardware setups. It provides a comprehensive suite of features for model deployment, including advanced attention mechanisms and distributed computing support.

documents.title: Learn More and Join the Community — SGLang
documents.summary: This document provides a directory of links for the SGLang community, including development roadmaps, meeting schedules, social media channels, and learning materials.

documents.title: Install SGLang — SGLang
documents.summary: This document provides comprehensive instructions for installing and deploying the SGLang library using various methods including pip, Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud platforms.

documents.title: SGLang Native APIs — SGLang
documents.summary: This document outlines the native server APIs for SGLang Runtime, providing a comprehensive list of endpoints for model interaction and server management alongside instructions for launching and testing the server.

documents.title: Offline Engine API — SGLang
documents.summary: This document describes the SGLang Offline Engine API for high-performance batch inference and custom server development without an external HTTP server.

documents.summary: This document explains how to launch and use an SGLang server with OpenAI-compatible embedding APIs to generate text embeddings using local models.

documents.title: OpenAI APIs - Vision — SGLang
documents.summary: This tutorial explains how to use SGLang's OpenAI-compatible vision APIs to serve and query vision language models locally. It provides instructions for launching the server and sending multimodal requests via cURL.

documents.summary: This document explains the architecture and operational workflow of HiCache, a multi-tier KV cache system for SGLang designed to optimize LLM inference. documents.title: DeepSeek V3.2 Usage
documents.description: DeepSeek-V3.2 model family equips DeepSeek-V3.1-Terminus with DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) through continued training. With DSA, a fine-grained sparse attention mechanism powered by a lightning indexer, DeepSeek-V3.2 achieves efficiency improvements in long-context scenarios.
documents.summary: This document provides comprehensive instructions for installing, configuring, and serving the DeepSeek V3.2 model family using SGLang, covering hardware-specific optimizations and advanced performance features.

documents.description: To serve GLM-4.5 / GLM-4.6 FP8 models on 8xH100/H200 GPUs:
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for serving GLM-4 series models using SGLang, including configurations for speculative decoding and implementing a thinking budget.

documents.title: GLM-4.6V / GLM-4.5V Usage
documents.description: Below are suggested launch commands tailored for different hardware / precision modes
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for deploying and optimizing GLM-4.6V and GLM-4.5V multimodal models using the SGLang framework, including server configuration and request handling for images and video.

documents.title: GPT OSS Usage
documents.description: Please refer to [https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/8833](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues/8833).
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide on using GPT-OSS with SGLang, detailing the Responses API, integration of built-in tools for Python and web search, and performance optimization through speculative decoding.

documents.title: Llama4 Usage
documents.description: [Llama 4](https://github.com/meta-llama/llama-models/blob/main/models/llama4/MODEL_CARD.md) is Meta's latest generation of open-source LLM model with industry-leading performance.
documents.summary: This document provides comprehensive instructions for serving Llama 4 models using SGLang, including configuration tips for hardware optimization, speculative decoding, and performance benchmarking.

documents.title: MiniMax M2.1/M2 Usage
documents.description: [MiniMax-M2.1](https://huggingface.co/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2.1) and [MiniMax-M2](https://huggingface.co/MiniMaxAI/MiniMax-M2) are advanced large language models created by [MiniMax](https://www.minimax.io/).
documents.summary: This document provides hardware requirements and deployment instructions for running MiniMax M2 and M2.1 models using SGLang, including configuration for multi-GPU setups and API testing.

documents.title: Ollama-Compatible API
documents.description: SGLang provides Ollama API compatibility, allowing you to use the Ollama CLI and Python library with SGLang as the inference backend.
documents.summary: This document explains how to use SGLang as a high-performance inference backend compatible with Ollama's CLI and Python library, including endpoint details and setup instructions.

documents.title: Qwen3-VL Usage
documents.description: [Qwen3-VL](https://huggingface.co/collections/Qwen/qwen3-vl) is Alibaba’s latest multimodal large language model with strong text, vision, and reasoning capabilities. SGLang supports Qwen3-VL Family of models with Image and Video input support.
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for deploying and optimizing the Qwen3-VL multimodal model using SGLang, including server launch commands, hardware-specific configurations, and API request examples for image and video processing.

documents.title: Qwen3-Next Usage
documents.description: SGLang has supported Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Instruct and Qwen3-Next-80B-A3B-Thinking since [this PR](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/pull/10233).
documents.summary: This document explains how to deploy and optimize Qwen3-Next models using SGLang, covering server launch procedures, Mamba cache configurations, and speculative decoding setup.

documents.title: Sampling Parameters
documents.description: This doc describes the sampling parameters of the SGLang Runtime. It is the low-level endpoint of the runtime. If you want a high-level endpoint that can automatically handle chat templates, consider using the [OpenAI Compatible API](openai_api_completions.ipynb).
documents.summary: This document details the low-level sampling parameters and configuration options for the SGLang Runtime's /generate endpoint. It provides a comprehensive reference for input formats, multimodal data handling, and core sampling settings used during inference.

documents.title: Bench Serving Guide
documents.description: This guide explains how to benchmark online serving throughput and latency using `python -m sglang.bench_serving`. It supports multiple inference backends via OpenAI-compatible and native endpoints, and produces both console metrics and optional JSONL outputs.
documents.summary: This guide explains how to use the sglang benchmarking tool to measure the performance, throughput, and latency of various LLM inference backends. It details configuration options for datasets, request rates, and concurrency to evaluate metrics like time-to-first-token and inter-token latency.

documents.title: Benchmark and Profiling
documents.description: Note that this is a simplified test script without a dynamic batching server, so it may run out of memory for a batch size that a real server can handle. A real server truncates the prefill into several batches, while this simplified script does not.
documents.summary: This document provides instructions and command-line examples for benchmarking and profiling SGLang performance across various modes, including latency, throughput, and server-side profiling using PyTorch Profiler.

documents.title: Contribution Guide
documents.description: Welcome to **SGLang**! We appreciate your interest in contributing. This guide provides a concise overview of how to set up your environment, run tests, build documentation, and open a Pull Request (PR). Whether you’re fixing a small bug or developing a major feature, we encourage following the...
documents.summary: This document provides comprehensive instructions for developers looking to contribute to SGLang, covering environment setup, testing, code styling, and the pull request process.

documents.title: Development Guide Using Docker
documents.description: (Optional - you can skip this step if you plan to run sglang dev container locally)
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for setting up an SGLang development environment using Docker and VSCode, covering both automated dev containers and manual container configuration.

documents.title: Development Guide for JIT Kernels
documents.description: We strongly recommend using `clangd` as the language server for JIT kernel development. For Ubuntu/Debian, you can download clangd from [apt.llvm.org](https://apt.llvm.org/). If you are using VS Code, we recommend installing the `clangd` extension for better IDE integration.
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide for developing and integrating just-in-time (JIT) kernels within the SGLang framework, covering environment setup, C++ utilities, and Python interfaces.

documents.title: Evaluating New Models with SGLang
documents.description: This document provides commands for evaluating models' accuracy and performance. Before open-sourcing new models, we strongly suggest running these commands to verify whether the score matches your internal benchmark results.
documents.summary: This document provides instructions and command-line examples for evaluating the accuracy and performance of LLMs and VLMs using SGLang benchmarking scripts.

documents.title: Install SGLang
documents.description: You can install SGLang using one of the methods below. This page primarily applies to common NVIDIA GPU platforms. For other or newer platforms, please refer to the dedicated pages for [AMD GPUs](../platforms/amd_gpu.md), [Intel Xeon CPUs](../platforms/cpu_server.md), [TPU](../platforms/tpu.md), ...
documents.summary: This document provides comprehensive instructions for installing SGLang through various methods including package managers, containerization, and cloud-based deployment platforms.

documents.title: AMD GPUs
documents.description: This document describes how run SGLang on AMD GPUs. If you encounter issues or have questions, please [open an issue](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues).
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide for configuring system settings and installing SGLang on AMD GPUs, specifically focusing on optimization for MI300X hardware.

documents.title: Contribution Guide
documents.description: Welcome to **SGLang**! We appreciate your interest in contributing. This guide provides a concise overview of how to set up your environment, run tests, build documentation, and open a Pull Request (PR). Whether you’re fixing a small bug or developing a major feature, we encourage following the...
documents.summary: This document outlines the procedures and requirements for contributing to the SGLang project, including environment setup, testing protocols, and coding standards. It provides a step-by-step guide for developers to submit pull requests and follow the project's development workflow.

documents.title: Best Practice on Ascend NPU
documents.description: This section describes the best practice data of mainstream LLM models such as DeepSeek and Qwen on the Ascend Npu. If you encounter issues or have any questions, please [open an issue](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues).
documents.summary: This document provides performance benchmarks and optimal configuration settings for deploying DeepSeek and Qwen models on Huawei Ascend NPU hardware.

documents.description: W4A8 Model weights could be found [here](https://modelers.cn/models/Modelers_Park/DeepSeek-R1-0528-w4a8).
documents.summary: This document provides configuration commands and environment settings for deploying DeepSeek models on Huawei Atlas 800I A3 NPUs using SGLang. It covers deployment strategies including mixed mode and prefill-decode disaggregation for optimized inference performance.

documents.description: Model weights could be found [here](https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-32B)
documents.summary: This document provides configuration examples and shell commands for deploying various Qwen3 model architectures on Atlas 800I A3 NPUs using the SGLang serving framework.

documents.title: Support Models on Ascend NPU
documents.description: This section describes the models supported on the Ascend NPU, including Large Language Models, Multimodal Language Models, Embedding Models, Reward Models and Rerank Models. Mainstream DeepSeek/Qwen/GLM series are included. You are welcome to enable various models based on your business requirem...
documents.summary: This document provides a compatibility matrix of supported Large Language Models, Multimodal models, and other AI architectures on Ascend NPU A2 and A3 hardware.

documents.title: SGLang installation with NPUs support
documents.description: You can install SGLang using any of the methods below. Please go through `System Settings` section to ensure the clusters are roaring at max performance. Feel free to leave an issue [here at sglang](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/issues) if you encounter any issues or have any problems.
documents.summary: This document provides comprehensive instructions for installing and configuring SGLang on Ascend NPU hardware, including dependency requirements, source/docker installation methods, and system performance optimizations.

documents.title: CPU Servers
documents.description: The document addresses how to set up the [SGLang](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang) environment and run LLM inference on CPU servers. SGLang is enabled and optimized on the CPUs equipped with Intel® AMX® Instructions, which are 4th generation or newer Intel® Xeon® Scalable Processors.
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for setting up the SGLang environment and running optimized LLM inference on Intel CPU servers with AMX support. It covers installation via Docker or source, configuration of environment variables, and how to launch the serving engine.

documents.title: NVIDIA Jetson Orin
documents.description: Before starting, ensure the following:
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for installing and running SGLang on NVIDIA Jetson Orin hardware, covering environment prerequisites, container-based setup, and optimized inference techniques.

documents.title: TPU
documents.description: SGLang supports high-performance TPU inference through the SGLang-JAX backend, which is specifically optimized for Google Cloud TPUs. The JAX-based implementation delivers exceptional throughput and low latency for Large Language Model (LLM) serving workloads on TPU hardware.
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive guide for deploying high-performance LLM inference on Google Cloud TPUs using the SGLang-JAX backend. It covers installation, system requirements, feature support, and detailed server configuration for optimized model serving.

documents.title: XPU
documents.description: The document addresses how to set up the [SGLang](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang) environment and run LLM inference on Intel GPU, [see more context about Intel GPU support within PyTorch ecosystem](https://docs.pytorch.org/docs/stable/notes/get_start_xpu.html).
documents.summary: This document explains how to set up the SGLang environment, install necessary dependencies, and run LLM inference and benchmarking on Intel XPU hardware.

documents.title: Custom Chat Template
documents.description: **NOTE**: There are two chat template systems in SGLang project. This document is about setting a custom chat template for the OpenAI-compatible API server (defined at [conversation.py](https://github.com/sgl-project/sglang/blob/main/python/sglang/srt/conversation.py)). It is NOT related to the c...
documents.summary: This document explains how to configure and override custom chat templates for the SGLang OpenAI-compatible API server using built-in names, JSON files, or Jinja templates.

documents.title: Environment Variables
documents.description: SGLang supports various environment variables that can be used to configure its runtime behavior. This document provides a comprehensive list and aims to stay updated over time.
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive reference of environment variables used to configure SGLang's runtime behavior, performance optimizations, and advanced feature sets.

documents.title: Troubleshooting and Frequently Asked Questions
documents.description: This page lists common errors and tips for resolving them.
documents.summary: This document provides troubleshooting steps for common issues like CUDA out-of-memory errors and server hangs, along with explanations for inference determinism in sglang.

documents.title: Choices Methods in SGLang
documents.description: This doc describes the choices methods supported by SGLang.
documents.summary: This document explains the various selection methods available for the choices primitive in SGLang, detailing how options are ranked and selected based on different log-probability normalization techniques.

documents.title: Learn More and Join the Community
documents.summary: This document provides a collection of links and resources to help users engage with the SGLang community and stay informed about the project's development roadmap.

documents.title: Deploy On Kubernetes
documents.description: This document is for deploying a RoCE network-based SGLang two-node inference service on a Kubernetes (K8S) cluster.
documents.summary: This guide explains how to deploy SGLang on a Kubernetes cluster for multi-node distributed inference using the LeaderWorkerSet (LWS) API and RoCE networking.

documents.title: LWS Based PD Deploy
documents.description: `lmsysorg/sglang:deepep`
documents.summary: This document provides instructions and Kubernetes manifest examples for deploying SGLang in a disaggregated prefill/decode architecture using the LeaderWorkerSet (LWS) operator.

documents.title: Multi-Node Deployment
documents.description: **Run 405B (fp16) on Two Nodes**
documents.summary: This document provides instructions and examples for deploying large language models across multiple nodes using SGLang, including configurations for Llama 3.1 and SLURM cluster integration.

documents.title: DeepSeekV32-Exp RBG Based PD Deploy
documents.description: For RBG installation, please refer to: https://github.com/sgl-project/rbg
documents.summary: This document provides a deployment guide and Kubernetes manifest for running DeepSeek-V3 with Prefill-Decode disaggregation using the Role-Based Group (RBG) controller and sglang.

documents.title: Post-Training Integration
documents.description: SGLang has become the de facto inference backend for modern LLM training frameworks, powering state-of-the-art models across the industry. From GLM-4.6 to Qwen3, leading models leverage SGLang's high-performance inference during reinforcement learning and post-training workflows.
documents.summary: This document outlines the features and integration capabilities of SGLang as a high-performance inference backend for large-scale post-training and reinforcement learning workflows.

documents.title: Production Metrics
documents.description: SGLang exposes the following metrics via Prometheus. You can enable it by adding `--enable-metrics` when you launch the server.
documents.summary: This document outlines the production metrics available in SGLang via Prometheus integration, detailing how to enable monitoring and the specific metrics tracked for performance analysis.

documents.title: Production Request Tracing
documents.description: SGlang exports request trace data based on the OpenTelemetry Collector. You can enable tracing by adding the `--enable-trace` and configure the OpenTelemetry Collector endpoint using `--otlp-traces-endpoint` when launching the server.
documents.summary: This document explains how to enable, configure, and extend request tracing in SGLang using OpenTelemetry and Jaeger for monitoring and performance analysis.

documents.title: Enabling cache for torch.compile
documents.description: SGLang uses `max-autotune-no-cudagraphs` mode of torch.compile. The auto-tuning can be slow. If you want to deploy a model on many different machines, you can ship the torch.compile cache to these machines and skip the compilation steps.
documents.summary: This document explains how to enable and share the torch.compile cache in SGLang to speed up deployments by skipping the compilation process across multiple machines.

documents.title: Classification API
documents.description: This document describes the `/v1/classify` API endpoint implementation in SGLang, which is compatible with vLLM's classification API format.
documents.summary: This document details the implementation and usage of the /v1/classify API endpoint in SGLang, which provides a vLLM-compatible interface for text classification and reward modeling.

documents.title: Diffusion Language Models
documents.description: Diffusion language models have shown promise for non-autoregressive text generation with parallel decoding capabilities. Unlike auto-regressive language models, different diffusion language models require different decoding strategies.
documents.summary: This document provides instructions on how to launch, configure, and query diffusion language models using the sglang framework and its REST API or Python client.

documents.title: Diffusion Models
documents.description: SGLang Diffusion is an inference framework for accelerated image and video generation using diffusion models. It provides an end-to-end unified pipeline with optimized kernels from sgl-kernel and an efficient scheduler loop.
documents.summary: This document introduces SGLang Diffusion, an inference framework for accelerated image and video generation, providing installation instructions and a detailed compatibility matrix. It outlines support for various diffusion models, hardware platforms including NVIDIA and AMD GPUs, and specific performance optimizations.

documents.title: Embedding Models
documents.description: SGLang provides robust support for embedding models by integrating efficient serving mechanisms with its flexible programming interface. This integration allows for streamlined handling of embedding tasks, facilitating faster and more accurate retrieval and semantic search operations. SGLang's ar...
documents.summary: This document explains how to deploy and utilize various embedding models in SGLang, including text, multimodal, and Matryoshka embeddings.

documents.title: Large Language Models
documents.description: These models accept text input and produce text output (e.g., chat completions). They are primarily large language models (LLMs), some with mixture-of-experts (MoE) architectures for scaling.
documents.summary: This document lists the supported Large Language Model (LLM) architectures and provides instructions for launching a model server and verifying implementation details.

documents.title: MindSpore Models
documents.description: MindSpore is a high-performance AI framework optimized for Ascend NPUs. This doc guides users to run MindSpore models in SGLang.
documents.summary: This document explains how to install, configure, and run MindSpore-based models using the SGLang framework specifically on Ascend NPU hardware.

documents.title: Use Models From ModelScope
documents.description: To use a model from [ModelScope](https://www.modelscope.cn), set the environment variable `SGLANG_USE_MODELSCOPE`.
documents.summary: This document explains how to configure SGLANG to use models from ModelScope by setting the SGLANG_USE_MODELSCOPE environment variable and provides server launch examples.

documents.title: Multimodal Language Models
documents.description: These models accept multi-modal inputs (e.g., images and text) and generate text output. They augment language models with multimodal encoders.
documents.summary: This document lists the supported multimodal language models in SGLang and provides instructions for launching a server with vision-capable models.

documents.title: Rerank Models
documents.description: SGLang offers comprehensive support for rerank models by incorporating optimized serving frameworks with a flexible programming interface. This setup enables efficient processing of cross-encoder reranking tasks, improving the accuracy and relevance of search result ordering. SGLang’s design en...
documents.summary: This document describes how to deploy and interact with rerank models in SGLang, covering both cross-encoder and decoder-only architectures including multimodal support.

documents.title: Reward Models
documents.description: These models output a scalar reward score or classification result, often used in reinforcement learning or content moderation tasks.
documents.summary: This document provides instructions for launching reward models via SGLang and lists supported model families for preference scoring and classification.

documents.title: How to Support New Models
documents.description: This document explains how to add support for new language models and multimodal large language models (MLLMs) in SGLang. It also covers how to test new models and register external implementations.
documents.summary: This document provides a comprehensive technical guide on how to integrate, test, and benchmark new language and multimodal models within the SGLang runtime environment.

documents.title: Transformers fallback in SGLang
documents.description: `sglang` can fall back to using models that are available in `transformers`. This works for most decoder-style language models and support for vision-language models is coming soon!
documents.summary: This document explains how SGLang can fall back to the Hugging Face Transformers library for model execution, including configuration steps and requirements for remote code support.
